title: barleria lanceolata subsp. lanceolata
intro: Barleria lanceolata subsp. lanceolata, also known as the 'Blue Ginger', is a flowering plant species native to Africa and the Indian subcontinent. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and treat respiratory problems, among others. In herbal preparations, Barleria lanceolata subsp. lanceolata is often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of ailments, from skin irritations to colds and flu.
